method_distinctions.1_vs_7: Method 1 (unstructured): global threshold — single cutoff across all layers, no fine-tuning. Method 7 (one-shot): per-layer threshold — each layer independently pruned, no fine-tuning. Isolates global vs per-layer thresholding strategy.
method_distinctions.3_vs_7: Method 3 (magnitude): per-layer L1 + 10-epoch fine-tune recovery. Method 7 (one-shot): per-layer L1, NO fine-tuning. Identical pruning criterion; only fine-tuning presence differs. This paired comparison cleanly isolates the value of recovery training.

fixes_from_v4.FIX_A_method3_vs_7: Method 3 (Magnitude) now includes a 10-epoch fine-tune pass after pruning. Previously Methods 3 and 7 were bit-for-bit identical. Method 7 retains zero fine-tuning as the one-shot baseline.
fixes_from_v4.FIX_B_lth_mask_scope: LTH mask reverted to Conv2d + Linear weights only (as in v3). v4's FIX-4 erroneously included BN affine params, which are small-magnitude by normalization dynamics, not low importance. Zeroing BN gamma to 0 kills entire feature maps; this is destructive and caused LTH accuracy to drop from 0.9334 to 0.9276. BN params are now rewound to theta_0 and retrained freely.
fixes_from_v4.FIX_C_inference_timing: inference_ms() now uses torch.cuda.synchronize() before/after the timed loop. Without synchronization, CUDA kernel launches return immediately and wall-clock timing reflects Python overhead, not GPU compute. Metric is flagged as indicative in all results.

retained_from_v4.FIX_2_iterative_sparsity: make_permanent() outside the pruning loop — masks accumulate correctly, reaching target sparsity.
retained_from_v4.FIX_3_taylor_naming: Method 4 is 'Taylor Sensitivity Pruning' (Molchanov et al. 2017), not 'Movement Pruning' (Sanh et al. 2020).
retained_from_v4.FIX_1_global_vs_layerwise: Method 1 uses global thresholding; Method 7 uses per-layer thresholding. Distinction retained and clarified.

_meta.flops_interpretation: Mask-based: dense FLOPs unchanged (graph unmodified). Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-backend support. Structured (#2): GFLOPs genuinely reduced — measured on rebuilt model. Comparison across types is NOT apples-to-apples for FLOPs; this is disclosed in the taxonomy.
_meta.structured_pruning_safety: Only internal Bottleneck width (conv1→conv2) is pruned. conv3, bn3, and downsample paths are untouched, guaranteeing residual-addition shape safety in all blocks.
_meta.taylor_sensitivity_method: Importance = (1/N) Σ |∂L/∂w × w| over N batches. Normalized to remove large-tensor bias. Ref: Molchanov et al. 2017.
_meta.lth_protocol: θ₀ saved at SEED=42 before training. θ_T = result of training θ₀ in this script (same trajectory). Mask from global magnitude pruning of θ_T — Conv2d + Linear weights only. BN affine params rewound to θ₀ and trained freely (not masked). Ticket = m ⊙ θ₀ retrained with frozen mask. Ref: Frankle & Carlin 2019.
_meta.inference_ms_caveat: Latencies use CUDA synchronization (FIX-C) but remain indicative. Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — any latency differences reflect measurement variance, not real speedup. Use a dedicated profiler (e.g. NVIDIA Nsight, torch.profiler) for publication-quality latency numbers.

1_unstructured.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
1_unstructured.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).

2_structured.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
2_structured.flops_note: TRUE structured pruning: 30% of internal Bottleneck channels physically removed (conv1+bn1+conv2 per block). conv3, bn3, and downsample are untouched — residual shapes guaranteed valid. GFLOPs are genuinely reduced and measured by thop on the rebuilt model.

3_magnitude.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
3_magnitude.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
3_magnitude.method_note: Per-layer L1 magnitude pruning with 10-epoch fine-tune recovery (FIX-A). Each layer independently pruned to 50% sparsity, then full dataset fine-tuning. Contrast with Method 7 (same pruning criterion, zero fine-tuning). Reference: Han et al. 2015 'Learning both weights and connections'.

4_taylor_sensitivity.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
4_taylor_sensitivity.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
4_taylor_sensitivity.method_note: Taylor first-order sensitivity pruning (Molchanov et al. 2017). Importance = (1/N) Σ |∂L/∂w × w| over N batches. Normalized to remove large-tensor bias. NOT to be confused with movement pruning (Sanh et al. 2020), which accumulates weight displacement from zero during training.

5_lottery_ticket.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
5_lottery_ticket.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
5_lottery_ticket.lth_note: Correct LTH protocol (Frankle & Carlin 2019). θ₀ saved at SEED=42 before any training. θ_T obtained by training θ₀ for 30 epochs in this run. Global magnitude mask at 50% sparsity applied to θ_T. Mask covers ONLY Conv2d + Linear weight tensors (FIX-B). BN affine params (γ, β) rewound to θ₀ and retrained freely — excluding them from masking prevents silent feature-map death. Winning ticket = m ⊙ θ₀ retrained for 30 epochs with mask enforced after every gradient step.

6_iterative.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
6_iterative.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
6_iterative.method_note: 3 rounds of global L1 pruning + fine-tuning. Per-round sparsity increment: 0.2063 (compounds to 50%). Mask buffers kept alive across rounds (make_permanent called once after loop). Fine-tune LR schedule: 1e-3 × 0.5^round per round.

7_one_shot.inference_ms_note: Single-sample latency with CUDA synchronization (FIX-C). Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — differences reflect measurement variance, not true speedup. Report as indicative only; use a dedicated profiler for publication.
7_one_shot.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
7_one_shot.method_note: Layer-wise L1 one-shot pruning. Each layer pruned independently to 50% sparsity in a single pass with NO recovery fine-tuning. Paired comparison with Method 3 (same criterion + fine-tuning) isolates the value of recovery training. Paired comparison with Method 1 (global threshold, also no fine-tuning) isolates global vs per-layer thresholding.
